kindergarten.national-kindergarten-description: Information about the national kindergarten curriculum
kindergarten.international-kindergarten-description: Information about the international kindergarten curriculum
kindergarten.national-kindergarten-intro-paragraph: At the KG National Section of Harvest International School, we proudly lay the first stones in the educational journey of our young learners. We believe that early childhood education is not merely the beginning of schooling, but the cornerstone upon which character, curiosity, and lifelong learning are built.
kindergarten.national-kindergarten-vision-paragraph: With a clear vision of excellence, our section is committed to providing a rich, inspiring, and nurturing learning environment where every child is encouraged to explore, discover, and grow with confidence. We strive to cultivate young minds that are not only academically prepared, but also creative, compassionate, and socially responsible.
kindergarten.national-kindergarten-team-paragraph: Guided by a dedicated team of passionate educators, we aim to instill strong values, encourage critical thinking, and develop the skills that empower our students to become confident individuals and future leaders. Our approach blends knowledge with creativity, discipline with joy, and learning with meaningful experiences.
kindergarten.national-kindergarten-outro-paragraph: At Harvest International School, we believe that every child holds remarkable potential. Our mission is to nurture that potential with care, excellence, and pride, ensuring that our students take their first educational steps with confidence, enthusiasm, and a deep love for learning.
kindergarten.international-kindergarten-intro-paragra: In the Foundation Stage at Harvest International Schools, we believe that the early years of education are the most important in a child’s learning journey. Our programme provides a safe, nurturing, and stimulating environment where young learners develop curiosity, confidence, and a love for learning.
kindergarten.international-kindergarten-departments-p: The Foundation Stage at Harvest International Schools follows both the British and American early years frameworks, ensuring that children receive a balanced, high-quality international education. Through play-based learning, hands-on activities, and guided exploration, students develop essential skills in language, mathematics, social interaction, creativity, and problem-solving.
kindergarten.international-kindergarten-team-paragrap: Our dedicated teachers at Harvest International Schools create engaging classrooms where children feel happy, secure, and valued. We focus on developing the whole child by encouraging independence, cooperation, and respect for others while building strong academic foundations.
kindergarten.international-kindergarten-outro-paragra: Through meaningful learning experiences, interactive activities, and strong collaboration with parents, Harvest International Schools aims to prepare our students for a successful transition to the next stages of their education. Our goal is to help every child grow into a confident, responsible, and enthusiastic learner who is ready to explore the world around them.
kindergarten.pre-kindergarten-intro-paragraph: At Harvest International Schools, our Playschool and Preschool programmes provide a warm, caring, and joyful environment where young children begin their first steps in education. We believe that early childhood is a time for exploration, discovery, and building confidence.
kindergarten.pre-kindergarten-approach-paragraph: Through play-based learning and hands-on activities, children develop important early skills in communication, social interaction, creativity, and independence. At Harvest International Schools, our classrooms are designed to encourage curiosity and imagination while helping children feel safe, happy, and supported.
kindergarten.pre-kindergarten-team-paragraph: Our experienced teachers at Harvest International Schools guide children through engaging activities such as storytelling, music, art, movement, and early learning games that support their physical, emotional, and cognitive development.
kindergarten.pre-kindergarten-outro-paragraph: At this stage, we focus on nurturing each child’s individuality while helping them build friendships, learn routines, and develop positive attitudes toward learning. The Playschool and Preschool programmes at Harvest International Schools create a strong foundation that prepares children for a smooth and successful transition into the Foundation Stage.
kindergarten.quote-by-ms-reham-sardina-head-of-kinder: - Reham Sardina, Head of KG Department

national.national-school-intro-paragraph: Harvest National School is built on a strong belief that quality education is the foundation for shaping confident, capable, and responsible individuals. Our program combines the strength of national curriculum with a modern educational approach that prepares students not only for academic success, but also for life.
national.national-school-commitment-paragraph: We are committed to providing an outstanding learning environment where excellence, discipline, and creativity go hand in hand. Our students are encouraged to think critically, ask questions, and actively participate in their learning journey while developing strong values such as respect, responsibility, and perseverance.
national.national-school-high-quality-paragraph: Our highly qualified and dedicated teachers work closely with every student, guiding them academically while nurturing their personal growth. Through engaging lessons, enriching activities, and continuous support, we strive to help each learner reach their full potential.
national.national-school-beyond-classroom-paragra: At Harvest National School, education goes beyond the classroom. We believe in developing the whole child through academic achievement, cultural awareness, extracurricular activities, and character building. Our goal is to prepare students who are confident in their knowledge, proud of their identity, and ready to contribute positively to their community and the world around them.
national.national-school-outro-paragraph: Together with our parents, we build a supportive partnership that ensures every child feels valued, inspired, and empowered to succeed.
national.quote-by-ms-aya-abdelgawad-national-scho: - Aya A. Abdelgawad, National School Deputy

british.vision-of-the-british-department-paragra:
Our vision is to provide an outstanding British education that inspires students to
become confident, responsible, and globally minded individuals. We aim to nurture lifelong learners who are equipped with the knowledge, skills and values needed to succeed in an ever-changing world.
british.vision-of-the-british-department-extensi: At our British Department, we strive to create a positive and supportive learning environment where every student is encouraged to explore their potential, think critically and develop a passion for learning.
british.mission-of-the-british-department-paragr:
Our mission is to deliver the British curriculum with excellence while supporting the holistic development of our students. We are committed to providing a balanced
educational experience that focuses not only on academic achievement but also on students’ emotional well-being, personal growth and creativity.
british.mission-of-the-british-department-extens: The British curriculum is delivered through internationally recognized and approved textbooks from Cambridge Assessment International Education, ensuring high academic standards and global recognition.
british.to-provide-intro: To provide our students with wider academic opportunities, we prepare them through three internationally recognized examination boards
british.to-provide-outro: This diversity allows us to offer flexibility while maintaining the highest academic standards.
british.core-values-intro: In the British Department, we believe in
core-values-list: Academic Excellence – Encouraging students to achieve their highest potential.
Respect and Responsibility – Promoting positive values and mutual respect.
Confidence and Independence – Helping students become confident learners and decision makers.
Creativity and Innovation – Supporting curiosity, creativity, and critical thinking.
Well-being and Care – Supporting students academically, emotionally, and socially.
british.why-choose-our-british-department-intro: Our department offers a distinctive learning experience through
why-choose-our-british-department-list: A well-structured British curriculum aligned with international standards.
Teaching through Cambridge-approved resources and materials.
Preparation through three major international exam boards to ensure flexibility and academic excellence.
A supportive and nurturing environment that cares for students’ educational, psychological, and social development.
A wide range of educational and recreational activities that enrich students’ learning experience.
A strong commitment to the continuous professional development of our teachers to maintain high-quality teaching standards.
british.final-thoughts-paragraph: We continuously strive to raise the standards of both our dedicated educators and our talented students, ensuring that our department remains a place where excellence, growth and opportunity thrive.
british.quote-by-ms-marwa-elsobky-british-school: - Marwa ElSobky, Head of CIPP C Lower Secondary

american.welcome-paragraph: Welcome to our American Department — a place where learning feels like home and every student is treated like family.
american.believe-paragraph: We believe that education is not only about academics, but about nurturing confident, kind, and responsible young individuals. In our American Department, we combine a strong, internationally recognized curriculum with a warm, supportive environment where students feel safe, valued, and inspired to grow.
american.classroom-paragraph: Our classrooms are filled with curiosity, creativity, and meaningful connections. Teachers work closely with students, guiding them academically while also supporting their personal development. We celebrate effort, encourage questions, and help every child discover their strengths and passions.
proud-to-offer-list: A well-balanced American curriculum that prepares students for university and beyond
Small community-focused classrooms where every child is known and supported
Engaging activities in arts, sports, leadership, and community service
Strong partnership with parents built on communication, trust, and shared goals
american.final-thoughts-paragraph: In our American Department, we believe that when school and family work hand in hand, children thrive. Together, we build not only successful students, but compassionate individuals ready to make a positive difference in the world.
american.goodbye-sentence: We look forward to welcoming you into our family.
american.quote-by-ms-salma-ehab-american-school-h: - Salma Ehab, Head of American Department

partners.schooleverywhere-description: SchoolEveryWhere is a comprehensive, massive web-based school management system designed to streamline the administration and daily operations of educational institutions, particularly those tackling multi-education systems like American, British, or National curricula, by centralizing all data and automating routine functions. This platform encompasses a wide array of core features, including online admissions, real-time attendance tracking, advanced Time Table Management, automated Grade Book and mark-sheet generation, and robust Financial and Fee Management with online payment integration. Furthermore, it enhances community engagement by providing secure, separate portals for administrators, teachers, students, and parents, facilitating instant communication via SMS and alerts. The system has established a significant presence in Egypt for over 10 years, successfully supporting the operational needs of more than 20 schools across the country, aiming to revolutionize efficient school management by connecting all key stakeholders and providing valuable operational insights through extensive reports.
partners.ucmas-description: Universal Concept of Mental Arithmetic System (UCMAS) is a globally recognized, scientifically-proven child development program designed for children aged 4 to 13 years, when brain development is at its peak. Since 1993, UCMAS has transformed the lives of over 3 million children across more than 80 countries worldwide through 5,000+ centers. At Harvest International School, we proudly offer UCMAS as an enrichment program to develop our students' cognitive abilities far beyond traditional mathematics education. The program uses the abacus, an ancient Chinese calculating tool, to develop extraordinary mental arithmetic capabilities. Children begin by learning to manipulate the abacus beads with both hands, which activates both hemispheres of the brain simultaneously—the left logical hemisphere for mathematical formulas and sequencing, and the right creative hemisphere for visualization and spatial memory. Through systematic training, students progress from physical abacus use to visualizing a mental abacus, storing over 450,000 abacus images in their minds by program completion. This visualization technique enables students to perform complex calculations—including multi-digit addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division—faster than calculators, often solving 10-digit arithmetic problems in seconds. The benefits of UCMAS extend far beyond mathematical ability. The program significantly enhances Concentration, as children learn to focus on multiple simultaneous processes while filtering out distractions. Observation skills are sharpened through rapid flashcard training, teaching students to capture information at a glance. Listening abilities improve through high-speed oral arithmetic exercises. Visualization and Imagination are developed as students create and manipulate mental images of the abacus. Memory, particularly photographic memory, is strengthened as children memorize bead patterns and momentarily retain each calculation image before producing answers. Speed and Accuracy improve as students are progressively challenged to deliver precise results in decreasing time intervals. Creativity flourishes as right-brain activation facilitates innovative thinking and problem-solving. Self-Confidence grows through continuous positive feedback from parents, teachers, and peers, as well as through participation in competitions and public demonstrations of their abilities. The program's impact is supported by research from Harvard University and has received ISO 9001:2008 certification, making UCMAS the first organization in the mental arithmetic industry to achieve this distinction. Students receive systematic training through professionally curated courses with daily 15-20 minute practice assignments that develop strong work habits and discipline. At Harvest International School, UCMAS students consistently demonstrate superior academic performance, enhanced learning abilities across all subjects, and increased confidence in tackling challenging problems—skills that prepare them for success not just in mathematics, but in all areas of life and future careers.

computerLab.title: Computer Lab Policy
rules: Can’t use computer labs except for computer lessons.
Avoid touching or handle any equipment –especially the computer screen- until they receive instructions.
Touch the keyboard lightly.
Put all materials and garbage in appropriate containers.
Avoid bringing food or other banned items into the computer.
Raise hands if they need help and wait patiently and quietly for help.
Respect the rights and belongings of classmates.
Avoid abusing the hardware, in case of any problem with hardware or software, they tell the instructor who will contact the computer lab technician.
Close all programs before leaving the lab.
Leave their places clean.
computerLab.responsibilitiesIntro: Students are responsible for all actions done on their computers:
responsibilities: Log only onto their own user accounts.
Safeguard their computers and save all their work.
Log off their computer accounts when they are finished.
Students will be charged a fine for any damage they cause to any equipment or materials in the computer lab.

library.title: Library
library.lessons: Library Lessons: We have weekly Library lessons schedule for both English & Arabic languages.
rules: Students may have (2) items checked-out under their names at a time.
Most books circulate for two weeks.
The books must be returned to the library in the same condition they were borrowed.
If damaged in any way, i.e., covers, pages damaged by having pictures cut out, or writing in the book, a student has to pay the replacement cost of the book.
Please place books in the book drop at the circulation desk.
No eating, drinking, chewing gum, etc., is allowed.
Proper respect toward other people, teachers, the books and other materials must be maintained at all times while in the Library.
The noise level in the library should not rise above a whisper.

canteen.title: School Canteen
canteen.intro: Our school provides a variety of nutritious foods and beverages to students and staff that enables them to make healthy food choicesAll nutritious food choices are displayed on front shelves and in an area that students and staff can easily see.
rules: Students are allowed to buy from the school canteen during break time only, except water is allowed at any time but with permission.
Students should line up when buying from the school canteen.
Students should throw litters in the litter baskets, not on the floor.
It is forbidden to take drinks in any glass, or metal containers; only disposable plastic or paper cups and containers are allowed.
Teachers on duty have to watch over these rules.

smartClasses.title: Smart Classes
smartClasses.description: Smart classes are available in KG Department and Elementary schools We all know how helpful it is to remember something that is taught visually to us rather than the one that is read through pages after pages.

sports.title: Sports
sports.intro: Physical Education in schools concerns the involvement of children in fitness , activities, sports, Health Education, and gymnastics . All are designed to encourage a healthier, more enjoyable lifestyle.Through all the sports facilities, its apparatus and equipment, HIS offers excellent conditions for practicing physical sports, going all out to meet the needs of each user, thus increasing the number and quality of the available sports.
gymRules: No running or ball playing in the GYM.
The hall and gym must be kept neat and clean.
No gum chewing, candy, foods, seeds, or drink in the gym before, during or after school.
Only sneakers are allowed on the GYM floor. They must be laced all the way and tied securely before entering the GYM.
Do not leave the GYM without permission.
swimmingPoolRules: Student may not be allowed to enter the swimming pool if he/she has suffered from any communicable diseases.
No one shall enter a swimming pool without first having passed through a shower bath and foot bath.
Swimmers must wear proper swimming suits when entering the pool.
Slippers must be thoroughly cleaned when entering pool areas.
Swimmers must follow the instructions of the venue in-charge or life guards on duty.
